item.title: Monday MEME dump to help you with your mental health
item.description:
1. The exact moment he unlock a fetish that would stay for him for the rest of his life
2. Such grace
3. That kid's laugh
4. BUSTA
5. REAL MAN
6. I bet you didn't expect this ending
7. Little guy helping out with the dishes
8
9. Simple way to win
10
11
12. Why are your friends so violent?
13. MANLY MEN
15. Thanks to the two people (Hi, Mom and Dad!) who made it to the end of this dump. When I'm not busy making questionable life choices on the internet, I've been pouring my soul into a little passion project. If you got a kick out of this dump, you're going to love my game: "Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse)." Think "The Stanley Parable" but with even more chaos and quirky characters such this little nihilist pancake. Can you imagine? A first-person game mixed with humor? Anyway, please wishlist: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="Linkify-element">https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ It would mean the world to me and my brother if you checked out the game and gave it a wishlist. It won't disappoint. I promise. Just, whatever you do, don't press the big red button!
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: Reclaiming "Weird"
item.description: I'm hoping that in time, the word "Weird" will no longer have connotations to alt-right, authoritarian couch-humpers. From the series "Tales Of Mere Existence" by Lev Yilmaz Help Lev make this series at https://www.patreon.com/LevniYilmaz

item.title: Too many leeches
item.description: Recently tried to get footage of the Australian olympic break dance routine. All I can find is commentary on it consisting of people talking over still images. I had the same problem a while back with the Hawk Tuah girl. It's annoying that the internet has just become a collection of reaction videos.

item.dc:creator: BosunMark

item.title: On Racism:
item.description: "Trav­el is fatal to prej­u­dice, big­otry and nar­row-mind­ed­ness, and many of our peo­ple need it sore­ly on these accounts. Broad, whole­some, char­i­ta­ble views of men and things can not be acquired by veg­e­tat­ing in one lit­tle cor­ner of the earth all one’s life­time." - Mark Twain Source: Zen Pencils #73 - 'Journey' https://www.zenpencils.com/comic/journey/
item.dc:creator: ruint

item.title: Humor: Cuando toda tu familia viene a tu ceremonia de graduación (SONIDO)
item.description: Google Translate.- Humor: When your whole family comes to your graduation ceremony (SOUND)
item.dc:creator: exploradoresciclos2018

item.title: things that took me from grim to grin
item.description:
jimmy o yang. seriously this sort of blurry, wildly awful lumping together with the best of intentions has happened to many friends. and Jimmy is always funny!
the funniest stuff I've seen with the line "I have a boyfriend"!
Aakash Singh.. he's uncanny the way he sizes up people! and that instant "ay, shut up" just cracked me up.
i honestly never knew Jamie Foxx was so funny. this clip just gets better as it goes on. i like Graham Norton, he brings out the quirkiness of his guests.
intergalactic dad jokes! this whispering just makes this so much better... the whole channel is inspired https://youtube.com/@plutopiaworld. thought some of you would dig it too. that's it. just wanted to share what made me grin despite having no luck with job hunting for some months and a family member being hospitalised. hope y'all find something to cheer you up too. eta: thanks for the award. send funny stuff if you can!
item.dc:creator: dicot

item.dc:creator: BlakeNorthcott

item.title: Prom Dress Fairy | Tammi’s Closet
item.description: Betty Vitale started Tammi’s Closet, an organization that gives free prom dresses to girls who can’t afford them, in memory of her daughter Tammi -- who was killed in 1996 -- to keep her name going. Now, as long as she has the dresses, girls in need won’t have to pay a penny to have a perfect prom night.
item.dc:creator: 60SecondDocs

item.title: Thank you for being a Friend.
item.dc:creator: BibiBlocksberg

item.title: I’m going to help regardless of what may come
item.description: My next door neighbor needs a new heart. He was told by one major university hospital he isn’t a good candidate. He will likely not receive a transplant. I have been sneaking over to mow his lawn, trim, and do basic maintenance he is in too poor health to do for himself. I have been there, with my kidney failure, lung issues, plus many more medical problems. I don’t ask for anything in return, as I have had help every time I have had such struggles. A feeling of obligation towards a debt I can never fully repay to those who helped me. He is originally from a southern state in the USA and wasn’t prepared for the snow and ice of the north. He and I chat about the snow, he is planning on buying a snowblower, I told him if he buys it, I’ll take care of his driveway for him. I gave my snowblower to a couple in need when I moved away, but was forced to move back. I fear he won’t make it through the winter as is. He is in very poor health. If I had the money to purchase one, I would and rather he just focus on his health. He is talking about a snowblower that is about $100, but I don’t think he realizes that particular model won’t be powerful enough to take care of the job. Doing what I can to try and save up closer to something in the $500-$800 range, but I am overdrawn in my checking only 4 days after payday. So it hasn’t gone well. No matter what happens, I’ll manage and help him as best I can. I just worry about the man’s health. I would rather he not waste his money on a machine that won’t be able to handle the task. I am afraid that, come winter, I will go out to find him passed out or dead in the snow, having tried to shovel it himself. Winter is months away, but… … anyways. So that’s where my head is at today. Mowed for him the other day and he was home to catch and thank me. $100 snowblower isn’t going to do much of anything where I live when it snows. He is a good man, younger than I thought too. I can only hope he gets a chance… no matter the case, I’ll do what I can.
